steps.title: Identify the gap in the passage
steps.content: Crowley has “several influential champions” including Bloom and Merrill who regard his work as extraordinary—Bloom has read Little, Big more than forty times and calls it a “masterwork”—yet Crowley remains “not as well known as the most commercially successful American writers.” This gap between critical devotion and commercial visibility is the key signal.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Figures who call a work a “masterwork” and devote themselves to reading it repeatedly would naturally want it to achieve greater commercial success than it currently enjoys.

This directly aligns with choice D. The other choices either make unsupported comparisons (A and C) or overgeneralize from the specific champions named in the passage to “literary critics” broadly (B).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
